title: lastreopsis nigritiana
intro: Lastreopsis nigritiana, also known as New Zealand fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Maori medicine, where it is used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves contain sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which are believed to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, contributing to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Lastreopsis nigritiana is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and can also be dried and made into a powder for use in herbal remedies.
